standard: Mẹ ơi thấy bà ấy leo mà không sợ bị ngã, mà sợ kiến cắn bà ấy.
A: Mom, I saw her climbing and she wasn't afraid of falling, but she was afraid of being bitten by ants.
B: Mom, seeing her climb without being afraid of falling, but I'm afraid ants will bite her.
C: Mom, I saw her climbing and she's not afraid of falling, but she's afraid of ants biting her.

standard: Tiền đâu mà lắm vậy bà con nhỉ?
A: Where did all that money come from, folks?
B: Where does all that money come from, everyone?
C: Where does all this money come from, folks?

standard: bạn gửi hỏi người ta gửi cho đồng bào lũ lụt miền Bắc chỗ nào người ta chỉ cho nhé
A: You can ask people where to send aid to flood victims in the North, and they'll show you.
B: You should ask them where to send [donations] to the flood-affected people in the North, and they will show you.
C: You ask them where to send donations for flood victims in the North, they'll show you the right place, okay?

standard: Lên lịch đi nào, qua giờ đang xem đây.
A: Let's schedule it, I've been watching it for ages.
B: Let's set a schedule; I've been watching this for an hour now.
C: Let's make plans for later, it's past the hour and I'm watching something right now.

standard: May mà em chưa giống thế này
A: Luckily, I don't look like this yet.
B: Luckily, I'm not like this yet.
C: It's a good thing I'm not like this yet.

standard: Lại một cái Tết không về, không phải không có tiền mà tại bận công chuyện
A: Another Lunar New Year away from home, not because I don't have money, but because I'm too busy with work.
B: Another Lunar New Year without going home; it's not that I don't have money, but because I'm busy with work.
C: Another Tet away from home, not that I don't have money but because I'm busy with work.

standard: Mỗi tỉnh làm một ga chứ.
A: Each province should have its own train station.
B: Each province should have one station, right?
C: Each province should build one station.

standard: Vòng xoay Duy Tân nhé, nhà mình lúc trước ở đấy mà.
A: The Duy Tan roundabout, my house used to be there.
B: Duy Tan roundabout, my family used to live there.
C: The Duy Tân roundabout, right? My house was there before.

standard: Uống cho lắm. Ô tô hay xe máy gì cũng lo phạt nặng. Mày gọi nhờ đi.
A: Drink too much. Whether you drive a car or a motorbike, you'll get a hefty fine. You call someone for help.
B: Drink as much as you want. Whether it's a car or a motorbike, they're all likely to be heavily fined. You should call someone for a ride.
C: I drank way too much. Whether it's a car or motorcycle, they all risk heavy fines. You go ahead and call someone for help.

standard: Vậy mà họ lại đưa tin gì đâu vậy nhỉ?
A: But what news did they even report?
B: And yet, what on earth were they reporting?
C: And yet, what kind of news are they reporting?

standard: nên về Than Uyên chơi một lần không vợ nhỉ?
A: Should I go to Than Uyên for a visit without my wife?
B: Should we visit Than Uyen once, honey?
C: Should I go back to Than Uyên for a visit once, wife?

standard: mày làm gì dại dột vậy, có gì từ từ tính
A: Why are you doing something so foolish? Let's think about it calmly.
B: Why did you do something so foolish? Let's take it slow and figure things out.
C: Why are you doing something so reckless? Take it slow and think it through.

standard: có được nằm không ấy nhỉ?
A: Can I lie down?
B: I wonder if lying down is allowed?
C: I wonder if I can lie down there?

standard: Sao nhìn như nồi cám thật nhưng mà đây là món gì vậy mọi người? Mình không biết thật.
A: It looks like a pot of animal feed, but what is this dish, everyone? I really don't know.
B: Why does it look like actual bran, but what dish is this, everyone? I really don't know.
C: It looks like a pot of pig feed but what dish is this, everyone? I really don't know.

standard: sắp xếp về trước nửa tháng để dọn nhà là vừa nhé con, chứ như năm ngoái dọn không kịp đấy.
A: It's best to arrange to come home two weeks in advance to clean the house, otherwise we won't have enough time like last year.
B: Arrange to come back half a month early to clean the house, otherwise, like last year, we won't be able to clean it in time, my child.
C: Come back half a month earlier to clean the house, that's just the right timing, dear, because last year we couldn't finish cleaning in time.

standard: quán này chill nhỉ?
A: This place is so chill, isn't it?
B: This place is chill, isn't it?
C: This place is so chill, right?

standard: 3 năm trời nhỉ.
A: Three years, huh?
B: It's been 3 years, hasn't it?
C: 3 years already, huh.

standard: Chuột chạy rầm rầm thấy sợ
A: The rats were scurrying around so loudly it was frightening.
B: The rats are running around noisily, it's scary.
C: A mouse ran by making a thudding noise, I'm scared

standard: hôm nào sắm cái này mới được
A: I'll have to buy this sometime.
B: I'll have to get one of these someday.
C: I need to get this one someday

standard: Ra bắt, tối về làm nồi canh chua.
A: Go out and catch them, then come home in the evening to make a pot of sour fish soup.
B: Go catch some, and tonight I'll make a pot of sour soup.
C: Went out to catch some, will come back in the evening to make a pot of sour soup.

standard: Tới nhà em rồi. Rẽ, rẽ anh ơi.
A: We've arrived at my house. Turn, turn, sir.
B: I've arrived at my house. Turn, turn here!
C: I've arrived at my house now. Turn, turn please.

standard: Giờ thì ngược lại. Khi yêu, em ỉa vẫn nói thơm. Giờ thì thôi.
A: Now it's the opposite. When we were in love, I'd say my poop smelled good. Not anymore.
B: Now it's the opposite. When in love, I'd even say my poop smelled good. Now, no more.
C: Now it's the opposite. When I was in love, even when I farted, you'd still say it smelled nice. Now, forget it.

standard: Này xứng đáng có 10 người yêu.
A: This guy deserves to have 10 lovers.
B: This deserves to have 10 lovers.
C: Hey, you deserve to have 10 people who love you.

standard: Cá này ăn không ngon đấy, bà con tránh ra để tôi.
A: This fish doesn't taste good, everyone please move aside so I can eat.
B: This fish doesn't taste good, everyone stay away and leave it for me.
C: This fish isn't good to eat, folks, get out of my way and let me [have it].

standard: nói vậy xem chứ anh em mình nộp 7 triệu bây giờ đấy
A: Let's just say we're paying 7 million now.
B: Just say that, because we are paying 7 million now.
C: I'm telling you, we're contributing 7 million right now.

standard: Ra ruộng coi họ làm gì chưa nghe nói trước trường tiểu học.
A: Going to the fields to see what they're doing—I hadn't heard anything about that before going to elementary school.
B: Go to the field to see what they are doing; I heard it was in front of the primary school.
C: Going to the fields to see what they're doing, I've never heard of this before elementary school.

standard: Cái chỗ này là cái chỗ cho mấy anh lên đỉnh là phải
A: This place is the perfect spot for guys to reach their climax.
B: This place must be for the guys to reach their peak.
C: This is the spot where you guys reach the top, for sure

standard: Vợ Chồng nhà này tình cảm nhỉ, mãi giờ mà đang bé.
A: This couple is so affectionate; they're still having a baby even now.
B: This couple is so affectionate; they're still like this even though they're grown.
C: This couple is so affectionate, still lingering around even though they're still young.

standard: đợt đi đã có cái nhảy xa này đâu?
A: There wasn't any long jump activity on that trip, was there?
B: Was this long jump thing even there during the trip?
C: We didn't have this long jump on that trip though.

standard: Bạn ơi, cho mình địa chỉ, mình có đoàn từ thiện xin cho chị mấy cái giường, tội quá
A: Hey friend, can you give me the address? I have a charity group that needs some beds for this woman; she's in such a terrible situation.
B: Hey, please give me the address; I have a charity group that wants to give her a few beds, it's so heartbreaking.
C: Hey, give me the address. Our charity group would like to donate some beds to her. Poor thing.

standard: Dì 2 bơi không? kem, Su đâu rồi bạn!
A: Auntie, are you going swimming? Kem, where's Su?
B: Does Aunt 2 swim? Where are Kem and Su, friend!
C: Is aunt 2 swimming? Kem, Su, where are you!

standard: Tôi ở Thanh Hóa nhưng tôi ở nông thôn, tôi thích hơn ở thành phố.
A: I live in Thanh Hoa, but I live in the countryside, and I prefer that to living in the city.
B: I live in Thanh Hoa, but I live in the countryside; I prefer it over the city.
C: I live in Thanh Hóa but in the countryside. I prefer the city more.

standard: Cái quán trong ngõ em hay ăn đó mới hợp gu ta.
A: That restaurant in the alley where I usually eat is more to my liking.
B: That shop in the alley where I often eat is more my style.
C: That shop in the alley that I always eat at, it just fits my taste.

standard: Khôn như thế này xóm chúng tôi đầy.
A: There are plenty of people this clever in our neighborhood.
B: Our neighborhood is full of people as clever as this.
C: Our neighborhood is full of people this smart.

standard: Lụt nhẹ nhìn cảnh bà con ta đi giăng lưới bắt cá cũng vui gì đâu nhỉ?
A: Even during a mild flood, watching our people cast their nets to catch fish isn't exactly a joyful experience, is it?
B: With light flooding, watching our people cast nets to catch fish is quite joyful, isn't it?
C: During mild flooding, watching our folks go cast nets to catch fish isn't really all that fun, is it?

standard: Không bỏ qua trò chơi nào, vậy mà vẫn chưa trúng.
A: I didn't skip any games, yet I still haven't won anything.
B: I didn't skip any games, yet I still haven't won.
C: I didn't skip any game, yet I still haven't won.

standard: khổ quá, bao nhiêu mồ hôi công sức, trời hại rồi, thương bà con quê hương tôi
A: It's so sad, all the sweat and hard work, and now it's all been ruined by fate. I feel so sorry for my fellow villagers.
B: How miserable, so much sweat and effort, the heavens have played a cruel trick, I feel so sorry for the people of my homeland.
C: It's so hard, all that sweat and hard work, the weather has ruined everything, I feel so sorry for my fellow villagers back home.

standard: Rồi tôi về có ai dắt tôi đi ăn không này?
A: When I get home, will anyone take me out to eat?
B: Then when I return, will anyone take me out to eat?
C: So when I get home, will anyone take me out to eat?

standard: xem có nhầm con nào trong đây không.
A: Check if I've mistaken any of them for one of these.
B: See if any of the ones in here are mistaken.
C: Check if any of the kids here are mixed up.

standard: Nó không có gì để mà đăng nữa đấy.
A: There's nothing left to post.
B: There is nothing left for me to post.
C: It doesn't have anything left to post anymore.

standard: Đi tìm miếng thịt
A: Go find a piece of meat
B: Going to look for some meat
C: Going to find some meat

standard: Đêm đấy, còn một thùng đấy là đủ rồi đấy.
A: That night, one more barrel would be enough.
B: That night, one more crate was enough.
C: That night, having just one more crate would be enough.

standard: bữa nay từ thiện đang được đề cao ấy!
A: These days, charity is highly valued!
B: Charity is being highly emphasized these days!
C: Charity is being highly valued these days!

standard: Chỗ này là chỗ nào mà tao không biết.
A: I don't know where this place is.
B: Where is this place that I don't know?
C: Where is this place? I don't know.

standard: không về nhà mà về nhà nghỉ?
A: Instead of going home, they went to a guesthouse?
B: Not going home, but going to a motel?
C: Not going home but going to a motel?

standard: Đây là ông ngoại tôi mà sao tự nhiên quay ông ấy rồi nói lung tung vậy. Ông ấy đạp xe tập thể dục thôi chứ ông ấy giàu lắm.
A: This is my grandfather, why are you suddenly filming him and saying all sorts of nonsense? He's just riding his bike for exercise; he's very rich.
B: This is my maternal grandfather, so why did you suddenly film him and say random things? He is just riding a bike for exercise, but he is actually very rich.
C: This is my grandfather, why did you suddenly film him and say random stuff like that? He's just cycling for exercise, he's actually very rich.

standard: Tôi mà liều như thế này thì có người yêu lâu rồi bạn ơi.
A: If I were this daring, I would have had a girlfriend a long time ago, my friend.
B: If I were that daring, I would have had a lover a long time ago, my friend.
C: If I were reckless like this, I would have had a partner long ago, my friend.

standard: Chạy gì kinh thế, trời đất quỷ thần linh ơi?
A: What kind of crazy running is that, for heaven's sake?
B: What on earth are you running so fast for, oh my god?
C: What's the big rush, good heavens?

standard: Quán nào ram ngon ngon, chứ chợ ta ram không ngon.
A: There are some restaurants that serve delicious fried spring rolls, but the ones in our market aren't good.
B: Which shop has good ram? The ones at our market aren't good.
C: I'm looking for a shop that serves good ram, because the ram at our local market isn't tasty.

standard: Biểu diễn chút làm gì căng?
A: What's the big deal about just a little performance?
B: Why be so tense over a little performance?
C: Why get all uptight about just a little performance?

standard: Dân chúng tôi nói là làm, không nói cũng làm luôn.
A: Our people are people of action; they say what they mean and do what they say, even without being told.
B: Our people do what we say, and even if we don't say it, we still do it.
C: Our people say it and do it. Even when we don't say anything, we still get it done.

standard: Trời mà màu này thì hiểu bão đến mức nào rồi.
A: If the sky is this color, you can imagine how serious the storm is.
B: If the sky is this color, you can imagine how severe the storm is.
C: The sky is this color—you can tell just how bad the storm is.

standard: Ăn tại gốc là ngon nhất.
A: Eating it at the source is the best.
B: Eating at the source is the best.
C: Eating it right at the source is the most delicious.

standard: Tôi gầy được giống như thế này thì ngon.
A: If I could get this thin, it would be great.
B: It would be great if I could get thin like this.
C: It would be nice if I could be thin like this.

standard: Có uống đâu, đi tốn mồi thôi.
A: I didn't drink anything, I just wasted some food.
B: I'm not drinking; I'm just going to eat the snacks.
C: We're not even drinking, let's just go spend money on food anyway.

standard: Chỉ ăn với chuối dưa hấu, xoài chứ không có ăn cơm với mận nhé
A: You only eat plums with bananas, watermelons, and mangoes; you don't eat them with rice.
B: Only eat with bananas, watermelon, and mangoes; don't eat rice with plums.
C: I'll only eat bananas, watermelons, and mangoes, but I won't eat rice with plums, okay?

standard: Không có cái nào tử tế cả các nàng ơi
A: None of them are any good, ladies.
B: None of them are decent, girls.
C: None of them are any good at all, girls

standard: Ở Sài Gòn mà chơi, ai cho ra đây
A: If you're going to have fun in Saigon, who would let you come here?
B: I'm playing in Saigon, who would let me come here?
C: Playing in Saigon, but who let you out here?

standard: chạy cho quen sau này trúng độc đắc đi xe hơi khỏi bỡ ngỡ
A: Get used to driving so that when you win the lottery and drive a car later, you won't be surprised.
B: Run to get used to it, so that if I win the jackpot later, I won't be overwhelmed when driving a car.
C: Practice running now so that when I win the jackpot and drive a car someday, I won't be caught off guard

standard: Cái đó chịu á. Cứ được giải là liên hoan à. Bù thiếu tính sau chứ em.
A: That's impossible. Every time we win an award, we have a party. We'll figure out the rest later, okay?
B: I can't deal with that. Every time there's a prize, we have a party. We can make up for the shortage later.
C: That's acceptable. As long as we get a prize, it's a celebration, right? We'll make up for the shortfall later, okay?

standard: Khoe làm gì vậy. Chúng mày biết, chúng mày tán tỉnh chồng tao thì sao?
A: Why brag? You know what? You're flirting with my husband, so what?
B: Why are you showing off? You know, what if you flirt with my husband?
C: Why are you showing off? You all know what would happen if you flirted with my husband, right?

standard: Ai thấy cá kho bên đấy đắt quá thì mua cá bên em xách theo ăn bánh căn nhé
A: If you find the braised fish over there too expensive, buy some from me and bring some along to eat with banh can (a type of Vietnamese pancake).
B: If anyone finds the braised fish over there too expensive, buy fish from me to bring along and eat with bánh căn.
C: Anyone who finds the braised fish over there too expensive, buy fish from me and bring it along to eat with bánh căn, okay?

standard: Bà con lo mà chạy trốn đi, chứ bão này mà vào thì nó quét tan sạch nhà cửa hết đấy.
A: People should hurry and run away, because if this storm hits, it will sweep away all the houses.
B: Everyone, please focus on escaping, because if this storm hits, it will wipe out all the houses.
C: Everyone, be worried and run away to safety, because if this storm hits, it will sweep away all the houses completely.

standard: ngay đối diện nhà, chếch lên tí nhỉ .
A: Right across from the house, just a little further up.
B: Right opposite the house, slightly offset, right?
C: Right across from my house, but a bit to the side.

standard: Bỏ phố về đi chăn trâu thôi
A: Let's leave the city and go herd buffaloes!
B: Let's leave the city and go herd buffaloes.
C: Let's just forget the city and go herd buffalo.

standard: Có vài đứa như 50 tuổi cố trang điểm để lấy tí màu.
A: Some people, like 50-year-olds, try to put on makeup to get a bit of color.
B: There are a few people who look like they're 50 and try to wear makeup just to get a bit of color.
C: Some people around 50 years old trying hard to put on makeup just to get some color.

standard: Hiệp một thua một trái tôi đi nhậu.
A: If we're losing by one goal in the first half, I'll go out for drinks.
B: If we lose by one goal in the first half, I'm going drinking.
C: First half, lost by one goal—I'm going for a drink.

standard: Con rắn gì mà nhìn thấy sợ nhỉ?
A: What kind of snake is that? It looks terrifying!
B: What kind of snake is it that looks so scary?
C: What kind of snake is so scary-looking?

standard: Em đây mà bồ bản là nơi nào em nhỉ, chị nhìn không ra em?
A: Where is that place you're looking for? I can't recognize you!
B: I'm here, but where is "bồ bản," dear? I can't recognize you.
C: I'm right here, but where are you that I can't recognize you?

standard: Đau ruột với chúng mày được đấy.
A: You guys could give me a stomachache.
B: I'm so fed up with you guys.
C: I can't stand you guys, honestly.

standard: lúc này là cái bữa mình thắc mắc này
A: This is the moment I'm wondering about this.
B: This is the time when I was wondering about this.
C: Right now is when I'm wondering about this

standard: Cầu dùng cho cả đoàn xe tăng đi qua mà anh chắc chắn mới là quân đội chứ.
A: The bridge was used by entire convoys of tanks, and you're sure it belonged to the military?
B: The bridge is used for an entire column of tanks to cross, and you're sure that's what makes it the military.
C: A bridge for an entire tank convoy to pass through, and you're sure it's the military, right?

standard: Vậy là ai ăn trộm lái rồi.
A: So, who stole the car and drove it?
B: So someone stole the driver.
C: So someone already stole and drove it away.

standard: màu sắc thì không giống nhưng view thì giống.
A: The colors are different, but the view is the same.
B: The colors are not the same, but the view is.
C: The colors aren't the same, but the view is.

standard: chị lớn nghèo nhường lại chị thứ đi.
A: The older sister, being poor, gave way to the younger sister.
B: The older sister is poor, so let her give way to the second sister.
C: The older sister is poor, so she lets the younger sister have it.

standard: Chứ mà đông như thế này địt phát thối um lên đấy, từ từ rồi về.
A: With so many people around, it'll stink up if you just defecate once. Take your time and go home.
B: With it being this crowded, if I fart, it'll stink up the whole place, so I'll take my time and then go home.
C: With this many people, it's going to start stinking up the place, let's take our time and head back.

standard: Ừ nhỉ đọc lộn, nói mai kêu long nữa chứ.
A: Oh right, I misread it. I said Long should be called tomorrow too.
B: Oh right, I read it wrong, and I even said I'd tell Long tomorrow.
C: Oh right, I misread. I'll let Long know again tomorrow.

standard: Gặp mình là đi tiếp chứ xanh đỏ gì cũng giống nhau thôi
A: Meeting me means moving on, whether it's green or red, it's all the same.
B: If you meet me, just keep going; red or green lights are all the same.
C: Whenever we meet, I just keep going anyway—green or red lights, they're all the same to me.

standard: Có cả con gái nữa kìa, tôi mà là bố mẹ mấy đứa này thì chắc về tôi vứt vào xay lúa luôn.
A: There are daughters too! If I were their parent, I'd probably throw them into the rice mill right away.
B: There are even girls there; if I were their parents, I would probably throw them into the rice mill as soon as they got home.
C: There are daughters too, if I were the parents of these kids, I'd probably throw them into the rice mill.

standard: làm trò gì khó coi thế
A: What kind of disgusting thing are you doing?
B: What kind of unsightly thing are you doing?
C: That's so embarrassing

standard: Thôi, tội quá đi ông cố nội
A: Oh dear, that's terrible, great-grandfather!
B: Stop it, that's just too pitiful, man.
C: Oh, I feel so sorry for great-grandfather

standard: Khi đó đi mây về gió, tháng về 2 lần chứ ai đi tàu này nữa nhỉ?
A: Back then, people traveled by boat, coming back twice a month; who would still take this ship then?
B: Back then, we traveled by wind and clouds, and the monthly salary came twice; who would take this train anymore?
C: At that rate, moving at a snail's pace and only running twice a month—who would even take this train anymore?

standard: Bạn ai thấy dáng quen, bộ đồ quen thì gửi cho anh đó coi cái cho ngại dùm cái
A: If anyone recognizes the figure and outfit, please send it to him so he can see it and feel a little embarrassed.
B: If anyone sees a familiar figure or familiar clothes, please send it to him so he can see it; I'm embarrassed on his behalf.
C: If anyone sees a familiar figure or outfit, please send it to him to look at, I'm too embarrassed to do it myself

standard: biết vậy hồi đấy để đẻ trứng vịt lộn ăn cho rồi.
A: If I had known that back then, I would have just laid fertilized duck eggs to eat instead.
B: If I had known, I would have just laid balut eggs to eat back then.
C: If I'd known, I would have let it lay balut eggs to eat back then.
